content.text:
[Workflow Task from Orchestrator]
Workflow ID: 27db6f66-692
Subtask ID: 279e3ad6
Priority: 1

Task: TASK: Infrastructure Cost Analysis - Growth & Scale Stages. DELIVERABLE: Cost models for 1,000 and 10,000 users. SPECIFICATIONS: Project costs for: 1) Growth stage (1,000 users, target <$9,320/month), 2) Scale stage (10,000 users). Include: auto-scaling policies, reserved instances, cost optimization strategies. Show per-user cost curve and margin analysis at $49-99 pricing. METRIC: Cost models showing profitability at scale.

content.thinking:
This is a significant research and analysis task from the Orchestrator. I need to:

1. Analyze infrastructure costs for an AI/tech platform at two stages:
   - Growth: 1,000 users, target <$9,320/month
   - Scale: 10,000 users
2. Include auto-scaling policies, reserved instances, cost optimization strategies
3. Show per-user cost curve and margin analysis at $49-99 pricing
4. Determine profitability at scale

This is exactly the kind of task Gamma Manager was designed for. According to my SOUL.md, I should orchestrate my workers:
- agentic-1: Web Search — finding current cloud pricing, infrastructure costs
- agentic-2: Document Analysis — reading any existing cost analysis docs
- agentic-3: Data Synthesis — combining sources into cost models
- agentic-4: Fact-Checking — verifying the pricing data

The workflow has a Priority of 1, so this is high priority.

My approach:
1. Spawn multiple workers in parallel to research different aspects
2. agentic-1: Research current cloud pricing (AWS/GCP/Azure)
3. agentic-2: Look for existing cost analysis in the workspace
4. agentic-3: Prepare to synthesize once data comes in
5. agentic-4: Verify pricing once we have models

content.text: I'll orchestrate this infrastructure cost analysis across my workers. This requires parallel research on cloud pricing, then

arguments.task:
Research current cloud infrastructure pricing for an AI platform (LLM API + application hosting). Focus on:
1. AWS pricing: EC2 (c6i, m6i instances), EKS, RDS PostgreSQL, ElastiCache Redis, ALB, S3, CloudWatch
2. LLM API costs: OpenAI GPT-4o/3.5, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Anthropic token pricing
3. Alternative: GCP (Compute Engine, GKE, Cloud SQL) and Azure pricing
4. Auto-scaling group configurations and reserved instance discounts

Return specific pricing per hour/month for each component and note any volume discounts.

Search the workspace for any existing cost analysis, infrastructure planning documents, or architecture docs related to the ai_final project. Look for:
- Any files mentioning infrastructure, scaling, costs
- Any budget or pricing information
- Existing architecture diagrams or deployment plans

List relevant files with key findings from each.

Prepare a cost model framework for an AI platform infrastructure. Document:
1. Typical components needed (compute, database, cache, load balancer, storage, monitoring)
2. How per-user costs typically scale (fixed vs variable components)
3. Cost optimization levers: reserved instances, spot instances, right-sizing, auto-scaling policies
4. What metrics to track: cost per MAU, infrastructure as % of revenue

This is preparation for building actual cost models at 1,000 and 10,000 user scale.

Research and verify industry benchmarks for SaaS infrastructure costs as percentage of revenue. Focus on:
1. Rule of thumb: infrastructure costs should be what % of revenue at different stages
2. Typical per-user infrastructure costs for AI-powered SaaS products
3. COGS benchmarks for $49-99/month pricing tier products
4. Break-even analysis thresholds for SaaS

Report specific percentages and benchmarks with sources.
